notes.note: Generally identified with Volumnia Cytheris, the freedwoman and celebrated mime-actress; Cicero rebukes her for failing in the courtesies owed to Terentia. The letter dates to early January 47 BC, written from Brundisium while Cicero awaited Caesar's pardon after Pharsalus.

notes.anchor: The day before the Nones of January
notes.note: The Roman calendar counted backward from three fixed points each month; the Nones of January fell on the 5th, so this dates the letter to 4 January (47 BC).
